subject: First Year Topology
instructions: For the first five problems, show all your work and support all statements. Answer problems 6–15 with complete definitions, complete statements, an example, or a short proof.

problems.text: Let \(p:E\to B\) be a covering map, and let \(p(e_0)=b_0\). Prove that the lifting correspondence \(\phi:\pi_1(B,b_0)\to p^{-1}(b_0)\) is surjective if \(E\) is path connected, and bijective if \(E\) is simply connected.
Show that a continuous map \(f:S^1\to S^1\) is homotopic to \(z^n:S^1\to S^1\) (given by \(e^{i\theta}\mapsto e^{in\theta}\)) for some integer \(n\in\mathbb{Z}\).
State and prove the Brouwer fixed point theorem for the unit disk, \(D^2\).

The connected sum of two surfaces \(S\) and \(S'\) is obtained by cutting out a disk from each surface and gluing the resulting surfaces along their circle boundaries: \(S\mathbin{\#}S'=(S\setminus D^2)\cup_{S^1}(S'\setminus D^2)\). Let \(T\) be the torus. Using the Seifert–van Kampen theorem on the natural decomposition into two pieces given by the connected sum, calculate \(\pi_1(T\mathbin{\#}T)\).
Prove that if \(X\) is path connected and \(x_0\) and \(x_1\) are two points of \(X\), then \(\pi_1(X,x_0)\) is isomorphic to \(\pi_1(X,x_1)\).
Show that the Tietze Extension Theorem implies Urysohn’s Lemma.
Let \(X\) be a topological space. Define what it means for \(X\) to be compact.
Let \(X\) be a completely regular space. Define the Stone–Čech compactification of \(X\).
Let \([0,1]^J\) be the set of all functions from a set \(J\) to \([0,1]\). Prove that if \(\mathcal{F}\) is a closed subset of \([0,1]^J\), then \(\mathcal{F}\) is compact.

Let \(A\subset X\). Let \(r:X\to A\) be a retraction, meaning \(r\) is continuous and \(r(a)=a\) for all \(a\in A\). Show that for any \(a_0\in A\), the induced map \(r_*:\pi_1(X,a_0)\to\pi_1(A,a_0)\) on the fundamental groups is surjective.
Let \(\gamma:[0,1]\to X\) be a loop with basepoint \(x_0\). Let \(\gamma^{-1}\) be defined by \(\gamma^{-1}(t)=\gamma(1-t)\). Show that \(\gamma*\gamma^{-1}\) is loop homotopic to the constant loop.
Describe a surface whose fundamental group is not abelian.
Let \(X\) and \(Y\) be topological spaces and let \(f:X\to Y\) be continuous and surjective. Show that if \(X\) is connected, then so is \(Y\).
